contents: From HoTT Require Import Basics Types.
From HoTT Require Import Basics Types.

(** * Mapping spaces between classifying spaces *)

(** The type of pointed maps [B G ->* B H] is known to be equivalent to the set [G $-> H]. In this file, we compute [Pi 0] and [Pi 1] of the general function type [B G -> B H]. *)

(** ** Connected components of [B G -> B H] *)

(** The set of representations is the set of conjugacy classes of group homomorphisms. *)

Definition groupreps (G H : Group) : Type
  := @Quotient (G $-> H) (conj_grp_homo).

(** We show that the set of connected components of [B G -> B H] is in bijection with [groupreps G H]. *)

(** [fmap B] sends conjugation to the identity map. Therefore, it induces a map [groupreps G H -> Trunc 0 (B G -> B H)]. *)

Definition idmap_fmap_grp_conj {G : Group} (g : G)
  : fmap B (grp_conj g) == idmap.

goals.conclusion: fmap B (grp_conj g) == idmap
fmap B (grp_conj g) == idmap
(fun b : ClassifyingSpace G => fmap B (grp_conj g) b = idmap b) bbase

forall x : G,
DPath.DPath (fun b : ClassifyingSpace G => fmap B (grp_conj g) b = idmap b)
  (bloop x) ?bbase' ?bbase'
(fun b : ClassifyingSpace G => fmap B (grp_conj g) b = idmap b) bbase
bbase = bbase

forall x : G,
DPath.DPath (fun b : ClassifyingSpace G => fmap B (grp_conj g) b = idmap b)
  (bloop x)
  (bloop g
   :
   (fun b : ClassifyingSpace G => fmap B (grp_conj g) b = idmap b) bbase)
  (bloop g
   :
   (fun b : ClassifyingSpace G => fmap B (grp_conj g) b = idmap b) bbase)

DPath.DPath (fun b : ClassifyingSpace G => fmap B (grp_conj g) b = idmap b)
  (bloop x)
  (bloop g
   :
   (fun b : ClassifyingSpace G => fmap B (grp_conj g) b = idmap b) bbase)
  (bloop g
   :
   (fun b : ClassifyingSpace G => fmap B (grp_conj g) b = idmap b) bbase)

PathSquare (bloop g) (bloop g) (ap (fmap B (grp_conj g)) (bloop x))
  (ap idmap (bloop x))
bloop g @ ap idmap (bloop x) = ap (fmap B (grp_conj g)) (bloop x) @ bloop g
bloop g @ ap idmap (bloop x) = bloop (grp_conj g x) @ bloop g
bloop g @ ap idmap (bloop x) = bloop (grp_conj g x * g)
bloop g @ bloop x = bloop (grp_conj g x * g)
bloop (g * x) = bloop (grp_conj g x * g)

Definition groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg `{ua : Univalence} (G H : Group)
  : groupreps G H -> Trunc 0 (B G -> B H).

goals.conclusion: groupreps G H -> Trunc 0 (B G -> B H)
groupreps G H -> Trunc 0 (B G -> B H)
(G $-> H) -> Trunc 0 (B G -> B H)
forall a b : G $-> H, conj_grp_homo a b -> ?Goal a = ?Goal b
(G $-> H) -> Trunc 0 (B G -> B H)
Trunc 0 (B G -> B H)
B G -> B H

forall a b : G $-> H,
conj_grp_homo a b ->
(fun f : G $-> H => tr (fmap B f)) a = (fun f : G $-> H => tr (fmap B f)) b
(fun f : G $-> H => tr (fmap B f)) a = (fun f : G $-> H => tr (fmap B f)) b
tr (fmap B a) = tr (fmap B b)

hypotheses.type: forall g : G, a g = grp_conj h (b g)

goals.conclusion: fmap B a = fmap B b

hypotheses.type: forall g : G, a g = grp_conj h (b g)

goals.conclusion: fmap B a == fmap B b

contents: lhs' exact (fmap2 (g:=grp_conj h $o b) B r).

goals.conclusion: fmap B (grp_conj h $o b) == fmap B b

hypotheses.type: forall g : G, a g = grp_conj h (b g)

goals.conclusion: fmap B (grp_conj h) $o fmap B b == fmap B b

hypotheses.type: forall g : G, a g = grp_conj h (b g)

goals.conclusion: (fmap B (grp_conj h) $o fmap B b) x = fmap B b x

hypotheses.type: forall g : G, a g = grp_conj h (b g)

contents: (** We first show that [groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg] is an embedding. *)

Definition isemb_groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg `{ua : Univalence} {G H : Group}
  : IsEmbedding (groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg G H).

goals.conclusion: IsEmbedding (groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg G H)
IsEmbedding (groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg G H)
IsInjective (groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg G H)

forall a b : G $-> H,
groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg G H
  (class_of (fun u v : G $-> H => conj_grp_homo u v) a) =
groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg G H
  (class_of (fun u v : G $-> H => conj_grp_homo u v) b) ->
class_of (fun u v : G $-> H => conj_grp_homo u v) a =
class_of (fun u v : G $-> H => conj_grp_homo u v) b

class_of (fun u0 v0 : G $-> H => conj_grp_homo u0 v0) u =
class_of (fun u0 v0 : G $-> H => conj_grp_homo u0 v0) v

hypotheses.type:
groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg G H
  (class_of (fun u0 v0 : G $-> H => conj_grp_homo u0 v0) u) =
groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg G H
  (class_of (fun u0 v0 : G $-> H => conj_grp_homo u0 v0) v)

goals.conclusion: (fun u0 v0 : G $-> H => trunctype_type (conj_grp_homo u0 v0)) u v

hypotheses.type:
groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg G H
  (class_of (fun u0 v0 : G $-> H => conj_grp_homo u0 v0) u) =

  (class_of (fun u0 v0 : G $-> H => conj_grp_homo u0 v0) v)

goals.conclusion: (fun u0 v0 : G $-> H => trunctype_type (conj_grp_homo u0 v0)) u v

hypotheses.type: Tr (-1) (fmap B u = fmap B v)

goals.conclusion: {h : H & forall g : G, u g = grp_conj h (v g)}

hypotheses.type: fmap B u = fmap B v

goals.conclusion: {h0 : H & forall g : G, u g = grp_conj h0 (v g)}

goals.conclusion: forall g : G, u g = grp_conj h (v g)

hypotheses.type: fmap B u = fmap B v

goals.conclusion: u x = h * v x * inv h

goals.conclusion: equiv_g_loops_bg (u x) = equiv_g_loops_bg (h * v x * inv h)

hypotheses.type: fmap B u = fmap B v

goals.conclusion: bloop (u x) = bloop (h * v x * inv h)

hypotheses.type: fmap B u = fmap B v

goals.conclusion: bloop (u x) = (bloop h @ bloop (v x)) @ bloop h^

hypotheses.type: fmap B u = fmap B v

goals.conclusion: bloop (u x) = (bloop h @ bloop (v x)) @ (bloop h)^

hypotheses.type: fmap B u = fmap B v

contents: (* [h] is defined by applying [equiv_g_loops_bg^-1] and [bloop] is the inverse of that function. *)

goals.conclusion: bloop (u x) = (ap10 p bbase @ bloop (v x)) @ (ap10 p bbase)^

hypotheses.type: fmap B u = fmap B v

goals.conclusion:
ap (fmap B u) (bloop x) =
(ap10 p bbase @ ap (fmap B v) (bloop x)) @ (ap10 p bbase)^

hypotheses.type: fmap B u = fmap B v

contents: (** When [Y] is connected, every function [X -> Y] is merely pointed, so [pointed_fun] is a surjection. *)

Instance issurj_pointed_fun_conn `{Univalence} {X Y : pType} `{IsConnected 0 Y}
  : IsSurjection (pointed_fun : (X ->* Y) -> (X -> Y)).

goals.conclusion: IsConnMap (Tr (-1)) (pointed_fun : (X ->* Y) -> X -> Y)
IsConnMap (Tr (-1)) (pointed_fun : (X ->* Y) -> X -> Y)
IsConnMap (Tr (-1)) (fun x : {f : X -> Y & f pt = pt} => x.1)

contents:
(** It follows that [groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg] is surjective.  By definition, we have a commutative diagram
<<
               fmap B             pointed_fun
        G $-> H   <~>   (BG ->* BH)  ---------->  (BG -> BH)
           |                                      |
  class_of |                                      | tr
           v                                      v
        groupreps G H ------------------> Tr 0 (BG -> BH)
                     groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg
>>
    To show that [groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg] is surjective, it suffices to show that this is true after precomposition with [class_of] and so we just need to show that the other three maps are surjective.  Rocq can prove these by typeclass search, with one hint for [tr]. *)

Definition issurj_groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg `{ua : Univalence} {G H : Group}
  : IsSurjection (groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg G H).

goals.conclusion: IsConnMap (Tr (-1)) (groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg G H)
IsConnMap (Tr (-1)) (groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg G H)

IsConnMap (Tr (-1))
  (fun x : G $-> H =>
   groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg G H
     (class_of (fun u v : G $-> H => conj_grp_homo u v) x))

contents: change (IsConnMap (Tr (-1)) (tr (n:=0) o pointed_fun o fmap (a:=G) (b:=H) B)).

goals.conclusion: IsConnMap (Tr (-1)) (tr o pointed_fun o fmap B)
IsConnMap (Tr (-1)) (tr o pointed_fun o fmap B)

hypotheses.type:
forall (A B0 : Type) (f : A -> B0),
IsConnMap (Tr 0) f -> IsConnMap (Tr 0.-1) f

contents: (* [tr] is in fact 0-connected, so we give this hint. *)

Instance isequiv_groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg `{ua : Univalence} (G H : Group)
  : IsEquiv (groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg G H).

goals.conclusion: IsEquiv (groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg G H)
IsEquiv (groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg G H)
IsConnMap (Tr (-1)) (groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg G H)
IsEmbedding (groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg G H)
IsConnMap (Tr (-1)) (groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg G H)
IsEmbedding (groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg G H)

contents:
Definition equiv_groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg `{ua : Univalence} (G H : Group)
  : (groupreps G H) <~> Pi 0 [B G -> B H, fun x => bbase]
  := Build_Equiv _ _ _ (isequiv_groupreps_to_pi0_map_bg G H).

(** ** The fundamental group of [B G -> B H] *)

(** We show that [Pi 1 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]] is equivalent to the centralizer of the image of [v]. *)

(** The map in one direction is induced by [fmap11_B] applied to the subgroup inclusion and [v]. *)

Definition b_centralizer_grp_image_to_map_bg {G H : Group} (v : G $-> H)
  : B (subtype_centralizer_subgroup (grp_image v)) -> (B G -> B H).

goals.conclusion: B (subtype_centralizer_subgroup (grp_image v)) -> B G -> B H
B (subtype_centralizer_subgroup (grp_image v)) -> B G -> B H

forall (g : subtype_centralizer_subgroup (grp_image v)) 
(h : G),
subgroup_incl (subtype_centralizer_subgroup (grp_image v)) g * v h =
v h * subgroup_incl (subtype_centralizer_subgroup (grp_image v)) g
h * v g = v g * h
v g * h = h * v g
v g * h = h * v g

hypotheses.type: forall h0 : H, grp_image v h0 -> centralizer h0 h

contents:
Definition centralizer_grp_image_to_loops_map_bg {G H : Group} (v : G $-> H)
  : subtype_centralizer_subgroup (grp_image v)
      -> loops [B G -> B H, fmap B v]
  := fun x => ap (b_centralizer_grp_image_to_map_bg v) (bloop x).

Definition centralizer_grp_image_to_pi1_map_bg {G H : Group} (v : G $-> H)
  : subtype_centralizer_subgroup (grp_image v)
      -> Pi 1 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]
  := tr o (centralizer_grp_image_to_loops_map_bg v).

Definition pi1_map_bg_to_centralizer_group_image `{ua : Univalence}
  {G H : Group} (v : G $-> H)
  : Pi 1 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]
    $-> subtype_centralizer_subgroup (grp_image v).

goals.conclusion: Pi 1 [B G -> B H, fmap B v] $-> subtype_centralizer_subgroup (grp_image v)
Pi 1 [B G -> B H, fmap B v] $-> subtype_centralizer_subgroup (grp_image v)

contents: (* It's enough to define a group homomorphism to [H] whose image is in the centralizer. *)

goals.conclusion: Pi 1 [B G -> B H, fmap B v] $-> H

forall x : Pi 1 [B G -> B H, fmap B v],
subtype_centralizer_subgroup (grp_image v) (?f x)
Pi 1 [B G -> B H, fmap B v] $-> H
H $<~> ?Goal
Pi 1 [B G -> B H, fmap B v] $-> ?Goal

contents: (* For the homomorphism to [H], it's enough to define a homomorphism to [LoopGroup (B H)]. *)

goals.conclusion: Pi 1 [B G -> B H, fmap B v] $-> LoopGroup (B H)

contents: (* And for that, [ap10] does the trick. *)

goals.conclusion: Pi 1 [B G -> B H, fmap B v] -> LoopGroup (B H)
IsSemiGroupPreserving ?grp_homo_map
Pi 1 [B G -> B H, fmap B v] -> LoopGroup (B H)
LoopGroup (B H)

hypotheses.type: Pi 1 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]

contents: strip_truncations; change (pointed_fun (fmap B v) = (fmap B v)) in p.

goals.conclusion: LoopGroup (B H)

hypotheses.type: fmap B v = fmap B v

goals.conclusion:
IsSemiGroupPreserving
  (fun p : Pi 1 [B G -> B H, fmap B v] =>
   Trunc_ind
     (fun _ : Trunc 0 (loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])) =>
      LoopGroup (B H))
     (fun p0 : loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]) =>
      ap10 p0 bbase)
     p)

Trunc_ind
  (fun _ : Trunc 0 (loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])) =>
   LoopGroup (B H))
  (fun p0 : loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]) => ap10 p0 bbase)
  (p * q) =
Trunc_ind
  (fun _ : Trunc 0 (loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])) =>
   LoopGroup (B H))
  (fun p0 : loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]) => ap10 p0 bbase)
  p *
Trunc_ind
  (fun _ : Trunc 0 (loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])) =>
   LoopGroup (B H))
  (fun p0 : loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]) => ap10 p0 bbase)
  q

hypotheses.type: Pi 1 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]

contents: strip_truncations; change (pointed_fun (fmap B v) = fmap B v) in p, q.

goals.conclusion:
Trunc_ind
  (fun _ : Trunc 0 (loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])) =>
   LoopGroup (B H))
  (fun p0 : loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]) => ap10 p0 bbase)
  (tr p * tr q) =
Trunc_ind
  (fun _ : Trunc 0 (loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])) =>
   LoopGroup (B H))
  (fun p0 : loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]) => ap10 p0 bbase)
  (tr p) *
Trunc_ind
  (fun _ : Trunc 0 (loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])) =>
   LoopGroup (B H))
  (fun p0 : loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]) => ap10 p0 bbase)
  (tr q)

hypotheses.type: fmap B v = fmap B v
fmap B v = fmap B v

goals.conclusion: ap10 (p @ q) bbase = ap10 p bbase @ ap10 q bbase

hypotheses.type: fmap B v = fmap B v
fmap B v = fmap B v

contents: (* Now we need to show that the image of our homomorphism is in the centralizer. *)

goals.conclusion:
forall x : Pi 1 [B G -> B H, fmap B v],
subtype_centralizer_subgroup (grp_image v)
  ((grp_iso_g_loopgroup_bg^-1$ $o
    {|
      grp_homo_map :=
        fun p : Pi 1 [B G -> B H, fmap B v] =>
        Trunc_ind
          (fun _ : Trunc 0 (loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])) =>
           LoopGroup (B H))
          (fun p0 : loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]) =>
           ap10 p0 bbase)
          p;
      issemigrouppreserving_grp_homo :=
        (fun p q : Pi 1 [B G -> B H, fmap B v] =>
         Trunc_ind
           (fun
              aa : Trunc 0 (loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])) =>
            Trunc_ind
              (fun
                 _ : Trunc 0
                       (loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])) =>
               LoopGroup (B H))
              (fun p0 : loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]) =>
               ap10 p0 bbase)
              (p * aa) =
            Trunc_ind
              (fun
                 _ : Trunc 0
                       (loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])) =>
               LoopGroup (B H))
              (fun p0 : loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]) =>
               ap10 p0 bbase)
              p *
            Trunc_ind
              (fun
                 _ : Trunc 0
                       (loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])) =>
               LoopGroup (B H))
              (fun p0 : loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]) =>
               ap10 p0 bbase)
              aa)
           (fun q0 : loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]) =>
            Trunc_ind
              (fun
                 aa : Trunc 0
                        (loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])) =>
               Trunc_ind
                 (fun
                    _ : Trunc 0
                          (loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])) =>
                  LoopGroup (B H))
                 (fun p0 : loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]) =>
                  ap10 p0 bbase)
                 (aa * tr q0) =
               Trunc_ind
                 (fun
                    _ : Trunc 0
                          (loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])) =>
                  LoopGroup (B H))
                 (fun p0 : loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]) =>
                  ap10 p0 bbase)
                 aa *
               Trunc_ind
                 (fun
                    _ : Trunc 0
                          (loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])) =>
                  LoopGroup (B H))
                 (fun p0 : loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]) =>
                  ap10 p0 bbase)
                 (tr q0))
              (fun p0 : loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]) =>
               ap10_pp p0 q0 bbase
               :
               Trunc_ind
                 (fun
                    _ : Trunc 0
                          (loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])) =>
                  LoopGroup (B H))
                 (fun p1 : loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]) =>
                  ap10 p1 bbase)
                 (tr p0 * tr q0) =
               Trunc_ind
                 (fun
                    _ : Trunc 0
                          (loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])) =>
                  LoopGroup (B H))
                 (fun p1 : loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]) =>
                  ap10 p1 bbase)
                 (tr p0) *
               Trunc_ind
                 (fun
                    _ : Trunc 0
                          (loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])) =>
                  LoopGroup (B H))
                 (fun p1 : loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]) =>
                  ap10 p1 bbase)
                 (tr q0))
              p)
           q)
        :
        IsSemiGroupPreserving
          (fun p : Pi 1 [B G -> B H, fmap B v] =>
           Trunc_ind
             (fun
                _ : Trunc 0 (loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])) =>
              LoopGroup (B H))
             (fun p0 : loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]) =>
              ap10 p0 bbase)
             p)
    |}) x)

subtype_centralizer_subgroup (grp_image v)
  ((grp_iso_g_loopgroup_bg^-1$ $o
    {|
      grp_homo_map :=
        fun p0 : Pi 1 [B G -> B H, fmap B v] =>
        Trunc_ind
          (fun _ : Trunc 0 (loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])) =>
           LoopGroup (B H))
          (fun p1 : loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]) =>
           ap10 p1 bbase)
          p0;
      issemigrouppreserving_grp_homo :=
        (fun p0 q : Pi 1 [B G -> B H, fmap B v] =>
         Trunc_ind
           (fun
              aa : Trunc 0 (loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])) =>
            Trunc_ind
              (fun
                 _ : Trunc 0
                       (loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])) =>
               LoopGroup (B H))
              (fun p1 : loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]) =>
               ap10 p1 bbase)
              (p0 * aa) =
            Trunc_ind
              (fun
                 _ : Trunc 0
                       (loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])) =>
               LoopGroup (B H))
              (fun p1 : loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]) =>
               ap10 p1 bbase)
              p0 *
            Trunc_ind
              (fun
                 _ : Trunc 0
                       (loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])) =>
               LoopGroup (B H))
              (fun p1 : loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]) =>
               ap10 p1 bbase)
              aa)
           (fun q0 : loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]) =>
            Trunc_ind
              (fun
                 aa : Trunc 0
                        (loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])) =>
               Trunc_ind
                 (fun
                    _ : Trunc 0
                          (loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])) =>
                  LoopGroup (B H))
                 (fun p1 : loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]) =>
                  ap10 p1 bbase)
                 (aa * tr q0) =
               Trunc_ind
                 (fun
                    _ : Trunc 0
                          (loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])) =>
                  LoopGroup (B H))
                 (fun p1 : loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]) =>
                  ap10 p1 bbase)
                 aa *
               Trunc_ind
                 (fun
                    _ : Trunc 0
                          (loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])) =>
                  LoopGroup (B H))
                 (fun p1 : loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]) =>
                  ap10 p1 bbase)
                 (tr q0))
              (fun p1 : loops (iterated_loops 0 [B G -> B H, fmap B v]) =>
               ap10_pp p1 q0 bbase
               :
               Trunc_ind
                 (fun
                    _ : Trunc 0

                  LoopGroup (B H))

                  ap10 p2 bbase)
                 (tr q0))

Definition equiv_pi1_map_bg_to_centralizer_group_image `{ua : Univalence}
  {G H : Group} (v : G $-> H)
  : GroupIsomorphism
    (Pi 1 [B G -> B H, fmap B v])
    (subtype_centralizer_subgroup (grp_image v))
  := Build_GroupIsomorphism _ _ _ (isequiv_pi1_map_bg_to_centralizer_group_image v).
(** As a corollary, we can compute the homotopy groups of any function type of the form [X -> B G], where [X] is a pointed connected type. *)

goals.conclusion: groupreps (Pi 1 X) G <~> Tr 0 (X -> B G)
groupreps (Pi 1 X) G <~> Tr 0 (X -> B G)
groupreps (Pi 1 X) G <~> Tr 0 (B (Pi 1 X) -> B G)

  (X : pType) (G : Group) `{IsConnected 0 X} (v : Pi 1 X $-> G)
  : GroupIsomorphism
      (Pi 1 [X -> B G, equiv_bg_pi1_adjoint X G v])
      (subtype_centralizer_subgroup (grp_image v)).
